description: Concerns over the safety of Bihar's Rs 64-crore Keni Bridge have surfaced due to exposed iron rods and damaged pillars, sparking fears of collapse. The Road Construction Department has launched an inquiry and is inspecting all state bridges to assess their condition and lifespan, with strict action promised if irregularities are found.

description: Following Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari's Delhi visit, the Centre has approved Rs 39,000 crore for West Bengal's water resources ministry schemes. Prime Minister Modi pledged support for central projects and economic development, aiming to boost welfare initiatives like Ayushman Bharat and rural employment. Discussions also covered border security with Home Minister Amit Shah.

description: Police arrested a 33-year-old man, Karthik, in Coimbatore for the kidnapping and murder of a 10-year-old girl. The girl went missing on Thursday and was found dead on Friday. Karthik allegedly abducted her on a two-wheeler. His aide, Mohan Raj, was also detained. Protests demanding strict action were held, and officials assured a thorough investigation.

Telangana woman found hanging; husband accused of staging suicide for Rs 1 cr insurance claim
description: A 29-year-old woman was found dead under suspicious circumstances in Khammam district, with family members alleging her husband murdered her for a ₹1 crore insurance policy. They claim the couple faced marital issues and financial distress. Police are investigating domestic violence allegations and the insurance angle, with no murder charges yet invoked.

Have you been told to take thyroid medicines forever? Read this first
description: Thyroid medication isn't always a lifelong sentence. Dr. Vaibhav Gupta explains that temporary inflammation can resolve, allowing for reduced or stopped treatment. However, autoimmune conditions like Hashimoto's often necessitate permanent hormone replacement.  Hyperthyroidism, conversely, aims for remission.  Sudden discontinuation of medication is dangerous; always consult your doctor for adjustments based on TSH tests.

title: Google co-founder Sergey Brin donates $500,000 to California group, and this is why
description: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is injecting significant funds into San Francisco's local politics, backing a business-friendly tax proposal while opposing a controversial "Overpaid CEO Tax."  This move, following his relocation from Silicon Valley, reflects Brin's broader campaign against wealth redistribution measures, which he links to his personal experience fleeing socialism.
